title: AA Similarity Criterion
description: If two triangles have two pairs of congruent angles, then the triangles are similar.
condition: Two triangles with two pairs of congruent corresponding angles
response: Conclude the triangles are similar
label: Principle / Model
example: If \(\angle AEB \cong \angle DEC\) and \(\angle BAE \cong \angle CDE\), then \(\triangle ABE \sim \triangle DCE\).
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Classify Scale Factor Relative to 1
description: Determine whether a dilation’s scale factor is between 0 and 1, equal to 1, or greater than 1 by comparing distances from the center to a point and its image.
condition: A dilation centered at O with a point P and its image P' shown; distances OP and OP' can be compared.
response: If OP' < OP, scale factor is between 0 and 1; if OP' = OP, scale factor = 1; if OP' > OP, scale factor > 1.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $OP = 6$ and $OP' = 3$, then $k = \frac{3}{6} = \frac{1}{2}$, which is between 0 and 1.

title: Compute Dilated Distance from Center
description: Find the distance from the center to an image point by multiplying the original distance by the scale factor.
condition: Given center O, original distance OP, and scale factor k.
response: Compute OP' = k \cdot OP.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $OP = 10$ cm and $k = \frac{1}{2}$, then $OP' = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 10 = 5$ cm.
notes: Applies for any real scale factor k.

title: Compute Scale Factor from Corresponding Sides
description: Determine the scale factor by forming a ratio of corresponding side lengths in similar figures.
condition: Two corresponding side lengths from similar figures
response: Form and simplify the ratio of corresponding sides
label: Principle / Model
example: If \(BC = 2\) and \(RS = 1\), then the scale factor is \(\frac{2}{1} = 2\).

title: Convert Diameter to Radius
description: Find the radius of a circle by dividing the diameter by 2.
condition: Given the diameter of a circle.
response: Compute radius as diameter ÷ 2.
label: Principle
example: If the diameter is $2.5$, then the radius is $\frac{2.5}{2} = 1.25$.
notes: Based on the definition that diameter equals twice the radius.

title: Dilate Coordinates About the Origin
description: Apply a dilation centered at the origin by multiplying each coordinate by the scale factor.
condition: A point (x, y) is to be dilated about the origin by scale factor k.
response: Compute the image as (kx, ky).
label: Principle
example: Dilate $(4, -6)$ by scale factor $\tfrac{1}{2}$: $(4, -6) \rightarrow (2, -3)$.

title: Dilation Preserves Angle Measure
description: Recognize that angle measures remain unchanged under a dilation.
condition: Angle in a figure and its image under dilation.
response: Conclude that the image angle measure equals the original angle measure.
label: Concept
example: If $\angle B = 55^\circ$, then $\angle B' = 55^\circ$ under dilation.

title: Dilation Preserves Parallel Lines
description: Recognize that corresponding line segments not passing through the center of dilation remain parallel after dilation.
condition: Two corresponding segments in a figure and its dilation.
response: Conclude the image segment is parallel to the original segment.
label: Concept
example: If $AD \parallel BC$, then $A'D' \parallel B'C'$ after dilation.
notes: Lines through the center map to themselves; others map to parallel lines.

title: Dilation Produces Similar Figures
description: A figure obtained from another by a dilation (and rigid motions) is similar to the original.
condition: A figure is transformed by a dilation with scale factor not equal to 1 (possibly preceded by a rigid motion).
response: Conclude the image and original figures are similar (not congruent unless scale factor = 1).
label: Concept
example: If \(\triangle ABC\) is dilated by scale factor 2 to form \(\triangle A'B'C'\), then \(\triangle ABC \sim \triangle A'B'C'\).
notes: Used in Q4 Part B to classify triangles as similar.

title: Dilation Scales Segment Lengths
description: Determine how segment lengths change under a dilation by multiplying by the scale factor.
condition: Segment of length $L$ and dilation with scale factor $k$.
response: Image segment length is $kL$; compare sizes using value of $k$.
label: Principle
example: If $BC = 10$ and $k = \frac{1}{5}$, then $B'C' = \frac{1}{5} \cdot 10 = 2$, which is shorter than $BC$.
notes: If $0<k<1$, image is shorter; if $k>1$, image is longer.

title: Identify Center and Scale Factor from Point Mapping
description: Determine the center and scale factor of a dilation given a preimage point and its image.
condition: Given two points X and X' where X' is a dilation image of X, and multiple candidate centers and scale factors.
response: Choose the center C such that C, X, and X' are collinear and compute k = CX'/CX.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $W$, $X$, and $Z$ are collinear and $WX' = 5 \cdot WX$, then the dilation is centered at $W$ with scale factor $5$.
notes: Requires recognizing collinearity and multiplicative distance relationship.

title: Identify Center of Dilation by Collinearity
description: The center of dilation lies on lines connecting corresponding points of the original and image figures.
condition: Two similar figures with known corresponding vertices.
response: Locate the intersection point of lines through corresponding vertices to identify the center of dilation.
label: Concept
example: If lines \(AA'\), \(BB'\), and \(CC'\) intersect at point \(J\), then \(J\) is the center of dilation.
notes: Applied to find center J in Q3.

title: Identify Similar Right Triangles from Shared Hypotenuse Line
description: Recognize that right triangles with horizontal and vertical legs whose hypotenuses lie on the same straight line are similar.
condition: Multiple right triangles have horizontal and vertical legs and their hypotenuses lie on the same straight line.
response: Conclude the triangles are similar.
label: Concept
example: If two right triangles share the same slanted hypotenuse line, then corresponding leg ratios (e.g., $\frac{12}{9}$ and $\frac{y}{x}$) are equal.
notes: Similarity follows from equal corresponding angles (AA similarity).

title: Image of a Point Under Dilation from a Center
description: To find the image of a point under dilation, move along the ray from the center through the point and multiply the distance by the scale factor.
condition: Center \(O\), point \(P\), and scale factor \(k\)
response: Locate point on ray \(OP\) at distance \(k \cdot OP\) from \(O\)
label: Principle / Model
example: If \(OP = 3\) and \(k = 2\), the image lies on ray \(OP\) at distance \(6\) from \(O\).

title: Inverse Scale Factor for Reverse Dilation
description: To reverse a dilation, use the reciprocal of the original scale factor with the same center.
condition: A figure is obtained by a dilation with scale factor \(\frac{1}{r}\) centered at a given point.
response: Apply a dilation with scale factor \(r\) centered at the same point to map back to the original figure.
label: Principle
example: If a figure is dilated by scale factor \(\frac{1}{3}\) about \((0,0)\), then dilating by scale factor \(3\) about \((0,0)\) maps it back.

title: Locate Image Under Dilation on a Ray
description: Under a dilation with center O, the image of a point lies on the ray from O through the point, with distance multiplied by the scale factor.
condition: Given center O, point P, and scale factor k.
response: Locate point P' on ray OP such that $OP' = k \cdot OP$.
label: Principle
example: If $OP = 2$ and $k=3$, then $OP' = 6$ on the same ray from $O$ through $P$.

title: Model Height and Shadow with Similar Triangles
description: Represent a real-world height and shadow situation as similar right triangles to find an unknown height.
condition: Two objects cast shadows under the same light source with known heights and shadow lengths.
response: Set up and solve a proportion relating height-to-shadow ratios.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Assumes same sun angle creates similar triangles.

title: Recognize Dilation by Collinearity and Proportional Distances
description: Determine whether one figure is a dilation of another centered at O by checking that corresponding points are collinear with O and distances are proportional.
condition: Two similar-looking figures labeled as preimage and image with a marked center O.
response: Conclude it is a dilation centered at O if each pair of corresponding points and O are collinear and all distance ratios from O are equal.
label: Concept
example: If for each vertex $A$ and $A'$, points $O$, $A$, and $A'$ are collinear and $\frac{OA'}{OA} = 2$, then the figure is a dilation centered at $O$ with scale factor 2.
notes: Used to select all diagrams that represent dilations.

title: Set Up Proportion Using Corresponding Sides
description: Form an equation equating ratios of corresponding sides in similar triangles.
condition: Two triangles are known to be similar and corresponding sides are identified.
response: Write a proportion equating the ratios of corresponding sides.
label: Principle / Model
example: From similar triangles: $\frac{12}{9} = \frac{y}{x}$ or $\frac{9}{20} = \frac{2x-5}{16}$.

title: Similarity via Reflection and Dilation
description: A sequence consisting of a reflection and a dilation can map one similar figure onto another.
condition: Two similar figures positioned as mirror images across an axis and differing by a constant scale factor.
response: Apply a reflection across the axis followed by a dilation with the given scale factor about the origin.
label: Principle
example: Reflect a figure across the x-axis, then dilate by scale factor \(\frac{1}{2}\) about the origin to match a smaller reflected image.

title: Simplify Numeric Ratio
description: Reduce or compute the value of a ratio of two numbers.
condition: A ratio of two numerical quantities is given.
response: Simplify or compute the ratio value.
label: Principle / Model
notes: May remain unsimplified if problem accepts equivalent ratios.

title: Solve a Proportion for a Missing Side
description: Use equivalent ratios in similar triangles to solve for an unknown side length.
condition: Proportion involving a missing side in similar triangles
response: Set up proportion and solve algebraically
label: Principle / Model

title: Test Similarity Using Side Ratios
description: Determine whether two figures are similar by checking if all corresponding side ratios are equal.
condition: Corresponding side lengths of two figures are given or can be computed.
response: Compare ratios of corresponding sides and conclude whether a single scale factor exists.
label: Concept
example: If $\frac{BC}{RS} = \frac{2}{3}$ but $\frac{AD}{QT} = \frac{3}{6}$, the ratios are not equal, so the figures are not similar.
